title: Welcome to Hanoi
description: Arrival in the thousand-year-old capital. After settling in, enjoy an orientation walk through the French Quarter and Old Quarter to absorb the city's unique atmosphere.
activities: Arrival and hotel check-in
Orientation walk through the Old Quarter
Evening at leisure

dining.note: A Hanoi institution recognized with a Michelin Bib Gourmand, specializing in the legendary turmeric-and-dill fish dish.

historyNote: Hanoi's layer-upon-layer history begins with its 1010 AD founding by the Ly Dynasty.

title: The Imperial & Scholarly Capital
description: A deep dive into Vietnam's statehood and scholarly traditions.
activities: Imperial Citadel of Thăng Long (UNESCO World Heritage Site)
Vietnam National Museum of History
Temple of Literature (Quoc Tu Giam)

dining.note: An elevated culinary journey at Gia, awarded one Michelin Star for its innovative approach to Vietnamese heritage flavors.

historyNote: The Thăng Long Citadel was the center of regional political power for 13 consecutive centuries.

title: Ancient Capitals & Karst Landscapes
description: A day trip south to the 'Halong Bay on Land'.
activities: Tràng An Landscape Complex (UNESCO World Heritage Site) by traditional sampan
Hoa Lư Ancient Capital (capital of Vietnam in the 10th and 11th centuries)
Return to Hanoi

dining.lunch: Ninh Binh specialties (Goat meat and elderberry)
dining.note: Explore Hanoi's diverse street food or hotel dining.

historyNote: Hoa Lư served as the capital of the Đinh and early Lê dynasties.

title: 70th Birthday in the Citadel of Hue
description: Fly to the imperial heart of Vietnam. Today marks the 70th Birthday celebration.
activities: Morning flight to Huế
Complex of Huế Monuments (UNESCO World Heritage Site)
The Imperial City and Purple Forbidden City
Birthday Celebration

dining.note: A private, refined banquet featuring Hue's famous Imperial Cuisine at the hotel's signature restaurant.

accommodation: Silk Path Grand Hue Hotel / Azerai La Residence
historyNote: Huế was the seat of the Nguyễn Dynasty, the last ruling family of Vietnam (1802–1945).

title: Tombs of the Emperors
description: Exploring the poetic end-points of the Nguyễn Emperors.
activities: Tomb of Tự Đức (the most poetic of the imperial tombs)
Tomb of Khải Định (a unique blend of Oriental and European styles)
Thiên Mụ Pagoda (the iconic seven-story icon of Huế)

dining.note: Dining in a restored mandarin residence for authentic provincial flavors.

accommodation: Silk Path Grand Hue Hotel / Azerai La Residence
historyNote: The imperial tombs were designed as living palaces for the afterlife.

title: Over the Pass to the Champa Coast
description: A scenic drive via the Hai Van Pass to the central coast.
activities: Drive via Hai Van Pass ('Sea Cloud Pass')
Da Nang Museum of Cham Sculpture (early 20th-century colonial origin)
Arrive at The Nam Hai for coastal relaxation

dining.note: Beachside dining at one of the world's finest resorts.

accommodation: Four Seasons Resort The Nam Hai
historyNote: The Cham Museum houses the world's largest collection of Champa sculpture, established by the EFEO in 1915.

title: The Trading Port & The Sacred Sanctuary
description: A day of contrasts: from the bustling port of Hội An to the spiritual silence of Mỹ Sơn.
activities: Hội An Ancient Town (UNESCO World Heritage Site) walk
Mỹ Sơn Sanctuary (UNESCO World Heritage Site) - Hindu temple ruins
Evening in Hội An

dining.note: A visit to Nén Danang, awarded a Michelin Green Star for sustainable gastronomy and farm-to-table excellence.

accommodation: Four Seasons Resort The Nam Hai
historyNote: Hội An was a major Southeast Asian trading port from the 15th to 19th centuries.

title: Modern History & Thailand Departure
description: Concluding the journey in the dynamic south before the Thailand handoff.
activities: Morning flight to Ho Chi Minh City (Saigon)
Saigon Central Post Office and Notre Dame Cathedral
War Remnants Museum / Independence Palace
Evening flight to Bangkok, Thailand

dining.note: A final Vietnamese meal before the evening handoff in Bangkok.

historyNote: The Independence Palace was the site of the end of the Vietnam War in 1975.
